title: Energy Price Spike
description: Fuel prices surge after an international dispute.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Subsidies soften the blow. Countries that subsidized fuel avoided mass bankruptcies.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: But subsidies hide the real cost and delay adaptation. Letting prices rise encourages conservation.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Conservation doesn’t matter if unrest erupts. High fuel prices historically trigger instability.

turns.speaker: economy
turns.text: Rationing ensures supply but hurts the economy.

turns.speaker: rights
turns.text: And rationing restricts freedom. People should choose how they use energy.

turns.speaker: security
turns.text: Freedom means nothing if the grid collapses. Stability must come first.
